text:
There once was a peculiar shopkeeper who lived atop a hill overlooking a vast field. Each afternoon, she'd whistle cheerily, counting owlets. Every morning she'd count owls instead.

One spring day, she noticed a nest full of fluffy babies near her window.

"Young owls!" she exclaimed. "Those little things don't know much about the world, do they?"

She'd sit there singing them lullabies she made up--"Twinkle-twinkle little owl!"

Every morning, she'd watch them grow. They didn't want to fly yet.

Finally, one autumn day, they did take flight.

"Goodbye!" she called as they took off. "Be careful out there!"

From that year onwards, you could hear her echoing owlish wisdom. Each year, a new bunch of young owls would return.

They'd sit her cups of sweet things. She never drank them all.

One day, she decided it was time to visit them every autumn instead
